events.description: Drop-in program. A creative art program where children explore paint, collage, natural materials, and lose parts. Focus is on creativity and self-expression while building fine motor skills. This program is co-sponsored with Agincourt Community Services Association (ACSA).

events.title: 🎓 Celebrate the Class of 2026! Bloor/Gladstone Toddler Time Graduation 🎓
events.description: They’ve read, they’ve sung, and they’ve grown so much! Join us as we celebrate the incredible achievements of our little ones who have successfully completed another fantastic year of Toddler Time. Let's send our toddlers off to their next big adventure with cheers, certificates, and smiles. Note: This is a special, registration-only event for our participating families. Please secure your spot by contacting the branch. We can't wait to celebrate with you!

events.title: Movie Screening: Moana 2 (2024)
events.description: Join us for a movie on the big screen! Bring a friend, and a snack! Moana 2 (2024) 1h40min Rated: G Moana 2  is a musical animated adventure where an older, wiser Moana reunites with Maui and a new crew to sail dangerous, uncharted waters of Oceania. Three years after her first journey, she must break an ancient curse to connect her people with the rest of the world. *This program is suitable for all ages; younger children must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Drop in!*

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. C’est un instrument développé il y a plus de 200 ans qui utilise la réflexion de la lumière sur des miroirs pour créer des motifs répétitifs envoutants. Dans cet atelier, les enfants feront leur propre kaléidoscope et seront exposés aux termes de réflexion et réfraction de lumière tout en étant captivés par la physique des scintillements des motifs sur les surfaces réfléchissantes. events.title: STEM Workshop with Relay Education - Water
events.description: Dive into our waterways and learn how we use water for drinking, sanitation, energy, and more. We talk about Indigenous understandings of water, and how cities and rural areas manage water supply in Canada. This workshop highlights the importance of the hydrological cycle. The hydrological cycle describes the movement and flow of water on Earth, and involves different stages such as evaporation, condensation, and precipitation. Defying police orders, the duo goes on the run through new city sectors, uncovering a massive conspiracy involving the displaced reptile population and the city's corrupt founding families.

This class is open to children ages 9 and up. Computers will be provided. For more information about this program please contact dihonline@tpl.ca.

events.description: Every other Saturday Cliffcrest will be screening a free family friendly movie! This months' movie: Hero Mode With her indie video game business on the brink of bankruptcy. Kate throws a Hail Mary and puts Troy, her teenage coding genius son, in charge of creating a video game to save the company. It's a dream come true for Troy. But quite the opposite for the company's disgruntled coders and their rival CEO..

events.title: Blocks Challenges

This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: LivingWorks SafeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks SafeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks SafeTALK certificat

events.title: Knitting Circle
events.description: Join Port Union Library's Knitting Circle. Chat and knit or crochet with a friendly group! You can also share your projects with others!  This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot.  No registration required.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Hub: Paper Globe Lamps
events.description: Join us for a fun, illuminating program. Use watercolour paints, markers, tissue, and Mod Podge to create a unique ambient paper lampshade (lit with an electric tealight).  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

events.title: Port Union Chess Club
events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Programming in Robotics This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Seniors Celebrating Seniors: A Musical Walk Down Memory Lane
events.description: Join Jerry and Peter for an engaging and nostalgic musical experience featuring a rich selection of beloved songs from the 1950s and 1960s. Accomplished multi-instrumentalists, they will perform on a variety of instruments including guitar, bass, violin, and accordion, bringing timeless classics to life. This interactive, one-hour program invites participants to reminisce and share personal memories connected to the music, fostering a warm and inclusive atmosphere.
